what_it_does: Exports a React component that renders a gear button which toggles a fixed, animated settings panel containing a Taste sub-component for adjusting motion/feel preferences.
observations: Uses useLayoutEffect to measure the gear button's bottom and set the panel's top position.
The panel is rendered with fixed positioning and animated via framer-motion's AnimatePresence.
Includes a scrim overlay that closes the panel when clicked.
Passes feel and setFeel props into the Taste sub-component.
Uses a stamp utility to attach address attributes to the root and gear elements.
Sets aria-label and aria-expanded on the gear button.
standouts: Measurement-based top positioning of the panel rather than relying on CSS anchors or breakpoints.
Viewport-anchored fixed panel design intended to avoid clipping on narrow screens.
Taste controls are hidden by default behind a single gear affordance.
conventions: React functional component with TypeScript prop types.
Imports animation tokens and types from a tokens module.
concerns: top is null on first open render until the layout effect runs, so the panel may flash without a computed top.
The scrim is a plain div with onClick but no role or keyboard semantics.
No Escape key handler or focus trap for the opened panel.
Panel width/right positioning is delegated to external CSS classes not visible in this file.
notes: A collapsible gear menu that surfaces taste controls on demand, positioning its fixed panel by measuring the trigger and anchoring to the viewport.
questions: How are the panel's right edge and max-width constrained in CSS?
What does the stamp utility return and how are the address strings consumed?
What is the full shape of MotionFeel and how does Taste render the controls?
Is there intended focus management when the panel opens or closes?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of keeping the surface near-textless and revealing a viewport-anchored, measured panel on demand matches the implementation.
apparent_themes: On-demand controls to reduce surface clutter
Measured, device-derived layout over static breakpoints
Motion and feel as configurable taste dimensions
Lightweight accessibility hints via ARIA attributes
intention_signals: Keep settings out of the resting product surface until the user explicitly opens them, then present a compact, animated, device-aware panel for adjusting typeface, pigment, and motion feel.
novelty: Combines a measured trigger offset with a viewport-anchored fixed panel, and decorates UI nodes with stamp address attributes.
contribution: Provides a self-contained, collapsible settings affordance with measured positioning and motion transitions that a fused system could retain as the pattern for on-demand taste controls.
summary_for_embedding: Settings.tsx exports a React component that toggles a fixed, viewport-anchored taste panel positioned by measuring a gear button, with framer-motion enter/exit animation and a scrim close target.
intention_for_embedding: To let users access typeface/pigment/motion preferences through a hidden-by-default gear affordance that avoids cluttering the product surface.

symbols.does: Exports a collapsible settings gear component that reveals a taste panel.
symbols.description: Settings is a React functional component that accepts feel and setFeel props. It manages open state, measures the gear button's bottom with useLayoutEffect to set the panel's top position, and renders an animated fixed panel containing a Taste sub-component along with a scrim to dismiss the panel.
